meetings.summary: Politically loaded meeting. 4 notices of motion (constitutional maximum), 5 member questions, 2 public questions. Key battlegrounds: potholes motion directly targets Reform election promise; care homes questions test day centre commitment; highways budget question from Conservative leader.

standing_orders_summary.friendly_amendment_rule: Motion mover may amend their own motion or accept an amendment without debate or vote
standing_orders_summary.motions_that_fall_rule: Any motion not disposed of at Full Council falls. Does not carry forward to next meeting.

motions.title: Green Waste Composting Site Expansion, Kirkham
motions.full_text: That planning permission be granted subject to the conditions set out in Appendix 'A'.
motions.opposition_strategy: Opposition will likely focus on the retrospective nature of part of the application, arguing it shows a disregard for planning rules. They may raise concerns about increased noise, dust, and traffic impact on local residents, particularly those on Parkinson Lane, despite the proposed conditions. Visual impact on the rural setting could also be a point of attack. County Councillor Rick Edwards (Reform UK) declared an interest in a previous planning item and is a key opposition member, making him a likely speaker.
motions.ruling_group_response: Emphasise that the site is well-established and the proposal aims to improve operations, not intensify them. Highlight the comprehensive conditions in Appendix A designed to mitigate environmental and amenity impacts, such as restricted operating hours, white noise alarms, dust control, and vehicle sheeting. Stress compliance with relevant planning policies and the economic benefits of processing green waste. The retrospective element is being addressed through this application.

amendment_predictions.amendment_angle: Propose additional, stricter conditions related to vehicle movements, noise monitoring, or a shorter operating period. Alternatively, propose refusal of the application based on amenity impact or the retrospective element.
amendment_predictions.counter: Argue that the proposed conditions are robust and sufficient, developed in consultation with relevant bodies. Explain that further restrictions would be unreasonable or impractical. If refusal is proposed, reiterate the planning merits and policy compliance of the application.

key_data_points: Site is existing green waste recycling and composting site
Conditions include 3m stockpile limit, restricted operating hours, white noise reversing alarms, dust control, 30-year Habitat Management and Monitoring Plan, and a landscaping scheme.

motions.title: Children's Care Home, Great Harwood
motions.full_text: That planning permission be granted subject to the conditions set out in appendix A relating to the following matters: time limits; approved plans and documents; use limited to a children's care home as proposed and no other use within Use Class C2.
motions.opposition_strategy: Opposition will likely amplify the concerns of the three objectors, focusing on potential impacts on residential amenity, such as increased traffic, noise, and the change to the character of the street. They may question the suitability of a residential area for such a facility, despite the policy support. County Councillor Ashley Joynes (Reform UK) represents Accrington South, which is geographically close and may speak.
motions.ruling_group_response: Emphasise strong national policy support for accommodation for looked after children, citing the 'Planning for accommodation for looked after children' statement. Highlight that the proposal is for a small-scale facility (up to two children) with ample off-street parking (6 vehicles) to minimise traffic impact. Stress that the conditions limit the use strictly to a children's care home, preventing other C2 uses. Note the absence of objections from Hyndburn Borough Council and LCC Highways Development Control.

amendment_predictions.amendment_angle: Propose refusal based on unacceptable impact on residential amenity or a lack of demonstrated local need. Alternatively, propose additional conditions related to a management plan detailing visitor numbers or staff shift patterns.
amendment_predictions.counter: Reiterate that the proposal aligns with national and local policy to meet the needs of looked after children. Argue that the impacts are minimal and manageable through the proposed conditions. If a management plan condition is suggested, state that such operational details are generally outside planning control, or that the existing management plan (received 14/10/2025) is sufficient.

key_data_points: Change of use from C3 dwelling house to C2 residential institution
For up to two young persons (11-17 years)
Up to two overnight carers, up to four day staff
Property has off-street parking for 6 vehicles
National policy encourages suitable accommodation for looked after children.

motions.title: Noting of Delegated Development Control Decisions
motions.full_text: The Development Control Committee is asked to note to the report.
motions.opposition_strategy: Opposition members may use this 'noting' item as a platform to raise broader concerns about the transparency or appropriateness of delegated decision-making. They might question specific decisions listed (e.g., Ryelands Community Primary School demolition, Clayton Hall Sand Quarry EIA screening) or use it to criticise the overall efficiency or quality of development control processes under the current administration.
motions.ruling_group_response: Acknowledge the report and its purpose to inform the committee of routine decisions made under delegated authority. State that all decisions are made in strict accordance with the County Council's Scheme of Delegation and relevant planning policy. Offer to provide detailed background on any specific decision questioned outside of the meeting, to avoid lengthy debate on a noting item.
key_data_points: Report lists 4 delegated decisions since the last meeting
Includes demolition, EIA screening requests, and security fence installation.

tactical_scenarios.scenario: Opposition attempts to prolong debate on the 'Noting of Delegated Decisions' report to criticise broader planning policy or specific past decisions.
tactical_scenarios.counter: The Chair should firmly guide the debate back to the purpose of the report, which is for noting. Remind members that specific detailed questions about past delegated decisions should be submitted in writing or discussed offline. Do not allow the item to become a general policy debate. Offer to provide further information outside the meeting. Keep ruling group responses brief and factual.
tactical_scenarios.standing_order: Motion debate: 30 min per motion, Mover: 5 min, Others: 3 min (apply strictly to maintain control)

tactical_scenarios.scenario: Public questions are submitted, potentially on the planning applications or other contentious local issues, consuming the allocated time.
tactical_scenarios.counter: Prepare concise, factual answers to anticipated questions related to the planning applications (Tarnbrick Farm, Harwood Lane) and any general planning concerns. Ensure answers address public concerns without opening up new lines of attack. The Chair should manage time strictly to ensure all questions are heard but not over-debated.
tactical_scenarios.standing_order: Public questions: 30 min total (Chair must enforce this limit)

tactical_scenarios.scenario: A member proposes a friendly motion or extended tribute to Jonathan Haine, Head of Development Control, on his retirement.
tactical_scenarios.counter: Support the motion. The Chair should lead this, acknowledging Jonathan's 30 years of service. Keep remarks concise and positive to maintain goodwill and avoid it being used as a platform for other issues. This is an opportunity for cross-party unity.
tactical_scenarios.standing_order: N/A (likely handled as a brief statement or a short, non-contentious motion)

time_management.recommended_time_saving: Ensure the Chair strictly enforces speaker time limits (5 min mover, 3 min others) and guides debate on the 'Noting of Delegated Decisions' report to be concise. Discourage lengthy discussions on points already covered in reports or irrelevant to the specific motion.

key_points: Raise concerns about noise, dust, traffic impact on Parkinson Lane residents.

speakers.name: Ruling Group Member

Emphasise conditions limiting use and lack of objections from key consultees.

speakers.name: County Councillor Ashley Joynes
key_points: Represent objectors' concerns about amenity, traffic, character.

speaker_allocation.agenda_item: Noting of Delegated Development Control Decisions

speakers.name: Ruling Group Member
key_points: Briefly introduce report as update on routine delegated decisions.
Confirm decisions made under proper authority.

speakers.name: County Councillor Liam Thomson
key_points: Potentially question transparency of delegated powers.
Raise general concerns about specific decisions listed (e.g., Ryelands, Clayton Hall).

media_risk_items: Children's Care Home (LCC/2025/0024) - sensitive nature, local amenity concerns could attract media.
Green Waste Composting Site (LCC/2025/0021) - retrospective element, environmental/amenity concerns.
